ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR OCT-23-2018 CONTINUED TO DEC-11-2018 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR FAILURE TO FILE PROOF OF SERVICE ON DEFENDANT(S) AND OBTAIN ANSWER(S), OR ENTER DEFAULT(S). NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-17-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALBERT MENDOZA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT VALIDLY SERVED MR. MENDOZA BY SUBSTITUTE SERVICE. THE COURT STRONGLY ENCOURAGES MR. MENDOZA TO MAKE A GENERAL APPEARANCE IN THIS CASE NOTWITHSTANDING THAT HE HAS NOT YET BEEN VALIDLY SERVED. ABSENT A VOLUNTARY GENERAL APPEARANCE BY MR. MENDOZA, THE COURT SUGGESTS THAT WELLS FARGO USE THE NOTICE AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT PROCEDURE AND, IF MR. MENDOZA FAILS TO ACKNOWLEDGE SERVICE, HE WILL BE LIABLE FOR WHAT COULD BE VERY SUBSTANTIAL COSTS OF SERVICE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons [Ccp 418.10]
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Albert Mendoza's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff Wells Fargo has not shown that it validly served Mr. Mendoza by substitute service. The court strongly encourages Mr. Mendoza to make a general appearance in this case notwithstanding that he has not yet been validly served. Absent a voluntary general appearance by Mr. Mendoza, the court suggests that Wells Fargo use the notice and acknowledgment procedure and, if Mr. Mendoza fails to acknowledge service, he will be liable for what could be very substantial costs of service. Prevailing party to prepare order.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
